title: Tuple Operations
description: Use built-in tuple methods and functions to analyze tuple data. Implement functions that count occurrences of a value, find the index of a value, compute the sum of numeric elements, and return a statistics tuple containing the minimum, maximum, and average of the tuple's values.
hints: Tuples have two built-in methods: .count(value) and .index(value).
The .index() method raises a ValueError if the value is not found — use a try/except to handle that and return -1.
Python's built-in sum(), min(), and max() all work on tuples of numbers.
Average can be calculated as sum(t) / len(t).

testCases.description: find_index returns -1 when value not found
count_value returns 0 when value is absent
